title: The Origin of Turtles
message: Production package prepared. Awaiting local render.

script.hook: Have you ever wondered where turtles came from?
script.takeaway: The turtle's shell evolved step by step over millions of years.
script.main_concept: Turtle shell evolution from early reptiles; fossil intermediates

beats.text: Have you ever wondered where turtles came from?
Turtles evolved from early reptiles over two hundred million years ago.
Their shells didn't appear all at once — they developed gradually through evolution.
Fossils show intermediate forms that help scientists trace the transition.
The turtle's shell wasn't invented overnight—it evolved step by step over millions of years.

sources: Benton & Donoghue (2007) — calibrating turtle origins in deep time
Lyson et al. — fossil intermediates in shell evolution (Eunotosaurus / Proganochelys lineage)
Modern paleontology consensus: shells assembled gradually over tens of millions of years
